skill.name: obsidian-vault
skill.description: Search, create, and manage notes in the Obsidian vault with wikilinks and index notes. Use when user wants to find, create, or organize notes in Obsidian.
skill.summary: Documentation skill for managing an Obsidian vault with wikilinks, index notes, and flat-file organization.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation-only file (SKILL.md) containing workflow instructions for managing an Obsidian vault. All 15 static findings were dismissed as false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding on line 3 is a misidentification of the description text. The 14 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are standard bash code blocks (find/grep) inside fenced markdown, used for searching markdown files in a specific vault path. No executable scripts, no network access, no data exfiltration patterns detected.

content.user_title: Organize and Search Your Obsidian Vault
content.value_statement: Managing a large Obsidian vault with hundreds of notes becomes hard without consistent structure. This skill teaches Claude and Codex the naming, linking, and search conventions to keep your vault organized and discoverable.
actual_capabilities: Search for notes by filename or content using find and grep commands
Create new notes following Title Case naming and flat-folder conventions
Find related notes by searching for wikilink references across the vault
Locate index notes that aggregate related topics
Add wikilinks to related notes at the bottom of new notes
limitations: Vault path is hardcoded to a specific WSL mount location
Only supports flat organization - no folder hierarchy guidance
Commands use Unix tools (find/grep) which require WSL or Linux

use_cases.title: Research vault management
use_cases.description: Organize AI research notes with consistent naming and wikilinks for easy cross-referencing.

use_cases.title: Personal knowledge base
use_cases.description: Maintain a searchable personal knowledge base with index notes linking related topics.

use_cases.title: Project documentation
use_cases.description: Create interconnected project notes that link to dependencies and related concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a note
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Obsidian vault for notes about [topic] and show me the filenames.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding existing notes on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Create a new note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new note called [Title] in my Obsidian vault about [topic], with wikilinks to related notes at the bottom.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new note following vault conventions

prompt_templates.title: Find backlinks
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all notes in my vault that link to [Note Title] using wikilink syntax.
prompt_templates.scenario: Discovering which notes reference a given note

prompt_templates.title: Build an index note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an index note for [topic] that lists all related notes in my vault using wikilinks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Aggregating related notes into a single index

output_examples.input: Search for notes about 'RAG' in my Obsidian vault
Find all notes linking to 'Skills Index'
Create a note about prompt engineering
output_examples.output: Created 'Prompt Engineering.md' in vault root with content and wikilinks to related notes at the bottom.

best_practices: Use Title Case for all note filenames to maintain consistency
Place wikilinks to related notes at the bottom of each note
Create index notes to aggregate related topics rather than using folders
Search the vault with grep before creating a new note to avoid duplicates
anti_patterns: Creating deep folder hierarchies when index notes would work better
Writing notes without wikilinks to related content
Using inconsistent naming conventions across the vault
Storing all notes at root without any index structure

faq.question: Where is the vault located?
faq.answer: The vault is at /mnt/d/Obsidian Vault/AI Research/ on WSL. This is a hardcoded path in the skill.

faq.question: Does this skill support folder organization?
faq.answer: No. The skill enforces a flat structure at the root level. Use index notes and wikilinks for organization instead.

faq.question: What naming convention should notes follow?
faq.answer: All notes must use Title Case. Index notes should include the word 'Index' in the filename.

faq.question: How do I find notes that reference a specific note?
faq.answer: Use grep to search for the wikilink pattern like [[Note Title]] across the vault directory.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code tools.

faq.question: Does this skill run any scripts or make network requests?
faq.answer: No. It is documentation only, providing find and grep command examples for manual use.
